title: Italian Night
sub: For two. The one you cook when it matters.

runsheet.task: Mousse: whip, cup, fridge. It needs the two hours, nothing else does.
Potatoes into the air fryer. Veg tossed and ready on a tray.
Bruschetta topping mixed. Do not toast the bread yet.
Build the spritz. Ice first, then the bitter, then soda. Hand it over.
Toast the bread, spoon, serve. This is the only thing they watch you do.
Chicken in the pan. Twelve minutes, and the smell does the work.
Plate wide and shallow. Sauce under the chicken, never over.

plating: Wide shallow bowls. Sauce pooled underneath, chicken on top, one basil leaf. Potatoes in a separate dish so the plate stays clean.
call_it: creamy Tuscan chicken - never mention the yogurt

title: Desi Night
sub: For two. Tastes like home, weighs like it doesn't.

runsheet.task: Tikka marinade on. Longer is better - this is the one thing you cannot rush.
Gulab jamun rolled and syruped. They sit happily.
Butter chicken gravy to the point where it only needs the chicken.
Rice on. One whistle. Forget about it.
Highball built, or chaas if they're not drinking. Both take ninety seconds.
Tikka into the air fryer while they're in the room. The char smell is the starter.
Chicken into the gravy, two minutes, done. Raita out of the fridge last.

plating: Tikka on a small plate with onion rings and a lime wedge. Butter chicken in a katori, rice separate. Raita in the smallest bowl you own.
call_it: butter chicken - the cashew base is nobody's business

title: Asian Night
sub: For two. Sticky, hot, and over in thirty minutes.

runsheet.task: FroYo bark into the freezer. It is the least work and the most surprising.
Chicken cubed, sauce whisked. Both sit in bowls looking organised.
Edamame in salted water, three minutes, chilli salt over. Snack while you cook.
Rice on.
Chicken in a hot pan. Sauce in at the end or it burns - this is the whole trick.
Sesame, spring onion, serve. Bark out of the freezer twenty minutes later.

plating: White plate, sauce pooled not tossed, sesame and spring onion scattered late. Bark snapped, not cut.

title: Mexican Night
sub: For two. The sizzle is the entire performance.

runsheet.task: Panna cotta set in the fridge. It needs four hours and zero attention.
Peppers and onion sliced. Chicken strips seasoned. Everything on one tray.
Guac mashed rough. Make it in front of them - it is a two-minute show.
Margaritas. Fresh lime only. No mix, no sugar rim, no argument.
Pan screaming hot. Chicken, then peppers. Carry the pan to the table still hissing.
They build their own. You sit down. That's the point of fajitas.

plating: Cast iron straight to the table on a board. Warm tortillas in a cloth. Everything else in small bowls.
call_it: fajitas - and let the pan do the talking

title: Taco Night
sub: Four to six. An assembly line, not a service.

runsheet.task: Chicken marinated. Sangria built and left in the fridge - it improves overnight.
Peppers and onions sliced. Corn charred and dressed. Everything into bowls, cling film on.
Guac last - it browns. Make it big, make it rough.
Sangria poured immediately. It buys you twenty minutes.
Chicken in batches, tortillas warmed in a dry pan. Everything to the table at once and step back.
Mug cakes - one per person, ninety seconds each, made while they watch.

plating: One long spread. Warm tortillas under a cloth so they stay warm. Nobody plates anything - that's the format.

title: Mezze Board
sub: Four to six. Almost all of it is made before anyone arrives.

runsheet.task: Hummus and tzatziki made. Both are better on day two. Bark into the freezer.
Chicken on skewers, marinating. Falafel mix rolled and resting.
Board assembled cold - dips, pita, olives, cucumber. Cover it.
G&Ts, or virgin mojitos. Board out. You are now free.
Falafel in the air fryer, souvlaki on the pan. Twelve minutes total, both hot at once.

plating: One big board, dips in the middle, everything else radiating out. Hot skewers land on it straight off the pan.

title: Kebab Platter
sub: Four to six. Your freezer does most of the work.

runsheet.task: Seekh kebabs batched and frozen. This is why you did that.
Kulfi into moulds. Tikka marinade on.
Raitas mixed. Onion rings sliced and soaked in cold water so they stay sharp.
Chaas for everyone, highballs for whoever wants one.
Air fryer runs in three rounds: kebabs, tikka, paneer pops. Serve each as it lands - nobody waits for the full platter.
Kulfi out, five minutes to soften, then cut.

plating: Serve in waves straight from the air fryer onto a warm platter. Onion, lime, raita permanently on the table.
